data.title: Municipal bonds offer a “triple threat” of benefits as tax exemption risks fade – Wells Fargo
data.description:
Wells Fargo Investment strategists have upgraded municipal bonds (NYSEARCA:TFI), (BATS:ITM), (BATS:MLN), (NYSEARCA:SHM) to “favorable” from “neutral,” citing their unique combination of stability, attractive yields, and tax advantages.

Risks to the elimination of the municipal-bond tax exemption are receding, making this an opportune time for investors to consider these securities, said Brian Rehling, head of Global Fixed Income Strategy at Wells Fargo Investment Institute. “We believe municipal bonds currently offer investors an attractive entry point.”

Muni bonds (NYSEARCA:TFI), (BATS:ITM), (BATS:MLN), (NYSEARCA:SHM), which are debt instruments issued by state and local governments to fund essential projects like schools and infrastructure, maintain their appeal largely due to their tax-exempt status. While there had been speculation that Congress might remove the municipal tax exemption as part of a spending reconciliation bill, this possibility is “extremely low,” said Rehling. This reassurance has contributed to renewed investor confidence in the municipal bond market.

The credit stability of municipal bonds represents another significant advantage for investors. Because these securities are backed by the taxing power of state and local governments, they have demonstrated impressive reliability with investment-rated general obligation bonds and essential-service revenue bonds showing a five-year cumulative historical default rate of less than 0.05% since the 1970s.

“Compare this with corporate bonds, where defaults are more frequent and downgrades can feel like navigating a minefield at times,” Rehling added.

Rising U.S. Treasury yields (US10Y), (US2Y), (US5Y) have created an environment of higher yields across the bond market, making municipal securities particularly attractive for income-oriented investors.

As of May 21, 10-year AA-rated munis are yielding around 3.5%, a significant increase from the 1% yields of recent years. This yield improvement is “a big reason we upgraded our guidance on municipal bonds on May 15, to favorable from neutral,” Rehling said.

However, he warned that municipal bond prices will fluctuate as interest rates change, especially for longer-dated maturities. Interest rates will be “flat to lower in the year ahead,” but with a volatile path getting there, he added.

For better credit stability, Rehling recommended that investors “stick to higher-rated general obligation and essential-service revenue securities.”

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow pare gains as court strikes down Trump's April tariffs
data.description:
Wall Street major averages pared most of its gains on Thursday as the rally driven by a court striking down President Trump's April tariffs seemed to fade.

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was +0.2%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.3%, and the Dow (DJI) was near flat.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was down 3 basis points to 4.44%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 4 basis points to 3.95%.

A federal trade court has struck down the “reciprocal” tariffs that Trump imposed on what he called “Liberation Day,” putting an immediate stop to the collection of a sweeping set of levies that the president imposed.

“We should say at the outset that this (the court ruling) is hardly the end of this story, as the administration are appealing this decision. But already, markets have seen a substantial rally in response,” Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

AI darling Nvidia (NVDA) rose 3.7% after the firm reported fiscal first-quarter results and guidance that topped expectations, despite the impact of added export control curbs to China.

“The tariff developments have overshadowed the previous day’s news, but sentiment also got a boost from Nvidia’s results after the U.S. close yesterday, as the company delivered upbeat sales guidance,” Allen added.

For now, the court ruling lifts a significant tax burden from US consumers and companies. However, the main outcome is uncertainty, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Looking towards the economic calendar now, there are a few macroeconomic data along with several Federal Reserve speakers lined up on Thursday.

U.S. Q1 GDP decline pared to -0.2% in second estimate.

Initial jobless claims jumped past consensus in the last week, while Q1 U.S. corporate profits dropped 3.6% Q/Q.

Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee and San Francisco Fed President Mary Daly, along with a few other Fed officials, will be speaking later in the day at different times and events. 

Wall Street ended lower Wednesday, with a noticeable downward move in the final hour of trading triggered by a report that the U.S. government had ordered chip designers to stop selling to China.

data.title: Electronic Arts releases the trailer for EA Sports College Football 26
data.description:
Electronic Arts (NASDAQ:EA) released the official reveal trailer for its EA Sports College Football 2026 videogame on Thursday.

EA Sports College Football 26 is set to launch worldwide on July 10 for PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S. The game features over 2,700 new plays, thousands of real college athletes, and more than 300 real-world coaches, each bringing their own unique playing styles and schemes. Key modes include Road to Glory, where players rise from high school recruit to Heisman legend, and Dynasty, which allows fans to build and manage their dream program, recruit talent, and navigate the modern college football landscape, including the transfer portal and NIL opportunities.

Of note, EA Sports College Football 26 boasts advanced AI, dynamic substitutions, and detailed fatigue and injury systems, while the broadcast experience is elevated by the return of legendary commentators like Chris Fowler and Kirk Herbstreit. The game also introduces new online features, cross-play support, and the ability to assemble dream rosters in College Football Ultimate Team. 

Pre-orders are available, with early access for EA Play members starting on July 7.

Watch the official College Football 26 reveal trailer.

Electronic Arts' (NASDAQ:EA) College Football video game series began in 1993 with Bill Walsh College Football and quickly became a staple for sports gaming, introducing features like Dynasty Mode and school-specific traditions. After being rebranded as NCAA Football in 1998, the franchise grew in popularity, paralleling the Madden NFL series and becoming a significant revenue driver for the parent company. While legal disputes over player likenesses led to a hiatus after 2013, the series quickly returned and grew to exceed $1 billion in net bookings in FY25.

data.title: Nvidia execs, including CEO Huang, adopt new trading plan
data.description:
• Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) executives, including CEO Jensen Huang, adopted a new trading plan to sell some of their holdings, the company disclosed late on Wednesday.
  • Huang, CFO Colette Kress and director A. Brooke Seawell will sell 6M, 500,000 and 1.1M shares in a new 10b5-1 trading plan, the company disclosed in its latest 10-Q filing. Huang adopted his plan on March 20 and it expires on Dec. 31.
  • Nvidia did not respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
  • 10b5-1 trading plans are widely used by various company executives to avoid the appearance of insider trading.
  • Huang last disclosed that he would sell some of his Nvidia holdings in July 2024.

data.title: NextDecade signs 20-year deal to sell LNG to Japan's JERA
data.description:
NextDecade (NASDAQ:NEXT) announced Thursday it signed a 20-year deal to supply JERA, Japan's biggest power generator, with 2M metric tons/year of liquefied natural gas from its Rio Grande LNG project's fifth liquefaction facility, subject to a positive final investment decision on Train 5.

"We have seen strong commercial momentum this year for Rio Grande LNG, which is helping us commercialize Train 5 toward a positive FID," Chairman and CEO Matt Schatzman said.

NextDecade (NASDAQ:NEXT) has been signing supply deals for the Brownsville, Texas, facility, which is under construction and has a potential capacity of 48M metric tons/year, to strengthen its position in the international market, including 20-year agreements with Saudi Aramco and TotalEnergies from the fourth liquefaction facility of the project.

Last year, Abu Dhabi National Oil Company acquired an 11.7% stake in phase 1 of NextDecade's (NEXT) LNG project, which included the first three liquefaction trains and a 20-year supply deal for the fourth train.

data.title: IBIT ETF sees surge in inflows as bitcoin nears all-time high
data.description:
The iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF (NASDAQ:IBIT), managed by BlackRock, has emerged as a powerhouse in the ETF world, drawing massive investor attention as bitcoin (BTC-USD) hovers near record highs. As of today, bitcoin (BTC-USD) is trading near $108.2K, just shy of its all-time high of $112K reached on May 22.

IBIT has seen an extraordinary surge in interest, with nearly $9B in net inflows over the past month. In fact, the fund has recorded inflows on 29 of the last 30 trading days, and has impressively avoided outflows for 32 consecutive sessions. This week alone, the ETF attracted $482M on May 28 and $411M on May 27. 

With $72B in assets under management, IBIT now ranks as the 23rd largest ETF in the U.S.—an astonishing feat for a product launched just one year ago. For comparison, the next youngest fund in the top 25 from an assets under management perspective is 12 years old. 

Year-to-date, IBIT is up 14.6%, fueled by both bitcoin’s (BTC-USD) price momentum and surging investor demand. As digital assets gain mainstream traction, IBIT’s rapid ascent underscores growing institutional confidence in cryptocurrency-based investment products. 

For reference, here are other spot bitcoin ETFs that may be worth monitoring: (BATS:ARKB), (NYSEARCA:GBTC), (NASDAQ:BRRR), (BATS:BTCO), (BATS:HODL), (BATS:BTCW), (BATS:FBTC), (NYSEARCA:BITB), and (BATS:EZBC).

data.title: First Trust Balanced Income ETF launched
data.description:
Exchange-traded funds provider and asset manager First Trust Advisors announced on Thursday the launch of the First Trust Balanced Income ETF (NYSEARCA:FTBI).

FTBI tracks the Bloomberg Moderate Allocation Income Focus Index, which is reconstituted and rebalanced semi-annually.

data.title: Performance Food Group gains again after its Investor Day presentation impresses
data.description:
Performance Food Group Company (NYSE:PFGC) traded higher for a second consecutive session after the company hosted its Investor Day event.

Highlights of the presentation from management included procurement savings, white space opportunities, e-commerce potential, and the upside in the specialty business.

In terms of guidance, Performance Food Group Company (NYSE:PFGC) said it continues to expect net sales to be in a range of $63 billion to $63.5 billion in FY25 and adjusted EBITDA to be in a range of $1.725 billion to $1.75 billion. As previously disclosed, the company's outlook for FY25 includes expected business results for Cheney Brothers as of the close of the transaction.

Looking ahead three years, the food company expects to achieve annual sales of $73 billion to $75 billion and adjusted EBITDA of $2.3 billion to $2.5 billion in FY28.

Notably, Performance Food Group's (PFGC) board authorized a new share repurchase program for up to $500 million. The authorization replaces the previously authorized $300 million share repurchase program

On Wall Street, BMO Capital Markets reiterated its Outperform rating on PFGC and $105 target price. The stock was called its top pick in the food distributor sector. UBS analyst Michael Lasser said the company laid out a compelling plan for growth. He also said Performance Food Group (PFGC) is achieving impressive results in quietly improving its technology offerings and back end systems, which the firm thinks will help with customer satisfaction over the long haul. 

Shares of Performance Food Group Company (PFGC) rose 1.0% in early trading on Thursday, after rallying more than 4% on Wednesday.

data.title: Talkspace rises on deal with Amazon Pharmacy
data.description:
• Talkspace (NASDAQ:TALK) said on Thursday that it is partnering with Amazon Pharmacy (NASDAQ:AMZN) to help its members fill their prescriptions through the provider and get faster, free home delivery.
  • In order to streamline the process, the deal lets the psychiatric provider of a Talkspace member send prescriptions directly to Amazon Pharmacy.
  • Through Amazon’s health benefits connector, shoppers can also confirm their insurance covers Talkspace therapy or psychiatry and book a virtual visit with a licensed provider, usually within 1 to 5 days, the company added.
  • TALK stock is up 14% in morning trade.
